hook.text: Overall metrics can hide the truth. Cohort analysis reveals it by grouping users by when they joined.

buildup.text: A cohort is a group of users who started using your product in the same week or month. You track each cohort separately.

discovery.text: If January's cohort retains at 40% but March's at 20%, something broke between January and March — even if overall numbers grew.

twist.text: Without cohorts, growing acquisition masks declining retention. Total users go up while individual experience gets worse.

climax.text: Cohort analysis is the single most important analytical technique in product management. It separates signal from noise.

punchline.text: Group users by when they joined. The truth lives in the cohorts.

quiz.question: Why is cohort analysis important?
options: It reveals retention trends that overall metrics hide — separating signal from noise
It's the fastest way to increase revenue
Cohort analysis only works for subscription products
